context: Tests whether the agent applies the correct lifecycle cadence table, correctly separates always-on flows from campaign calendar items, includes subscription-box specific monthly touchpoints (cycle reminder), includes segment declarations per campaign, and applies blackout/conflict rules for send timing.

checklist.name: Pre-launch cadence
checklist.description: Pre-launch period shows email cadence of 1 per week (not more)

checklist.name: Launch month cadence
checklist.description: Launch month shows email cadence of 2-3 per week

checklist.name: Post-launch cadence
checklist.description: Post-launch period (months 2-6) shows cadence of 1-2 per week

checklist.name: BFCM cadence
checklist.description: BFCM season (November) shows cadence of 4-7 per week (not lower)

checklist.name: Flows vs campaigns distinction
checklist.description: Output clearly distinguishes always-on flows (welcome, abandoned cart, post-purchase, etc.) from time-bound campaign calendar items

checklist.name: Monthly cycle reminder
checklist.description: Calendar includes a cycle-reminder send around the 10th of each month for subscription box

checklist.name: Segment declared per campaign
checklist.description: Each campaign row/entry in the calendar specifies which audience/segment receives it (not just 'all subscribers')

checklist.name: Sunday/Monday blackout rule
checklist.description: Calendar respects the rule to avoid Sundays before noon and Mondays before 9am as send times, OR the cadence rules section mentions these blackout times

checklist.name: Daily cap rule
checklist.description: Cadence rules section mentions capping at 1 send per recipient per 24 hours (with BFCM exception noted)

checklist.name: BFCM warm-up in September
checklist.description: Calendar includes a BFCM warm-up or save-the-date type send in September (not just starting BFCM prep in October or November)

checklist.name: What's NOT in calendar section
checklist.description: Output includes a notes section listing items deliberately excluded from the calendar and why
